rationale_for_baseline_tier: This is an acute neurotoxic decompensation of a known inborn error of metabolism with NEW extrapyramidal signs (bilateral resting tremor, dystonic posturing), hyperreflexia and 48 hours of escalating agitation and insomnia. A telehealth service cannot examine the patient, measure the phenylalanine level, or safely administer sedation; the decompensation needs urgent same-day in-person assessment by the metabolic team or emergency department for phenylalanine measurement and supervised management. It is not yet at the 000 threshold (no seizure, altered consciousness, airway compromise or violence), so T4 (urgent in-person, hours) is correct, WITH explicit T5/000 escalation edges. Under-triage to home management or routine GP risks progression to seizures and irreversible neurological injury; this is why the minimum viable tier is T4.

escalation_edges.rationale: Seizure signals severe neurotoxicity and is an immediate threat to life — call 000.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he has a fit or convulsion, call 000 immediately, protect his head, do not put anything in his mouth, and tell them he has a metabolic condition.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Tremor so severe he cannot hold a cup, or worsening stiffness/rigidity
escalation_edges.rationale: Marked worsening of the extrapyramidal signs indicates escalating neurotoxicity requiring emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the shaking gets so bad he can't hold a cup, or he stiffens up, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: He becomes aggressively violent toward himself or others
escalation_edges.rationale: Risk to the patient or others from an acute behavioural crisis driven by neurotoxicity — emergency response needed.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes violent and you cannot keep him or others safe, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Drowsiness, confusion or reduced responsiveness (altered consciousness)
escalation_edges.rationale: A falling conscious level signals a worsening encephalopathy — an immediate emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he becomes drowsy, confused or hard to wake, call 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vomiting and unable to keep the medical formula down
escalation_edges.rationale: If he cannot take the formula the metabolic clearance stalls and levels keep rising — he needs urgent in-person care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he vomits and can't keep the formula down, take him to the emergency department urgently.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: No improvement or continued escalation despite resuming the formula
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Within hours if not already assessed
escalation_edges.rationale: Persistent or worsening neurotoxicity requires urgent metabolic-team review regardless of the initial disposition.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he does not start to settle, he still needs to be seen urgently in person today.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: 16-year-old (adolescent) — paediatric; no weight-based sedative dose may be given remotely
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Sedation is an in-person decision by the metabolic/ED team; telehealth focuses on recognition, formula resumption, and urgent transfer.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Intellectual disability may limit his self-report of symptoms
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold for in-person assessment; rely on caregiver observation and escalate readily rather than seeking self-report.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Exhausted caregivers managing an unmanageable adolescent at home
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear, specific escalation triggers and arrange urgent in-person support; do not leave the family to manage the crisis alone.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: He needs to be seen in person urgently today. Arrange immediate assessment by his metabolic team or the nearest emergency department for a phenylalanine blood level and supervised care. In the meantime, give his prescribed phenylalanine-free medical formula now, and give him no normal protein at all — no meat, dairy or normal bread.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Contact the metabolic team or present to the emergency department today; call 000 if any red-flag sign appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it is after hours or the metabolic team is unavailable, go to the nearest emergency department. 000 is available 24 hours for any emergency sign — seizure, collapse, inability to hold objects, altered consciousness, or violence.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the metabolic team and GP with the timeline of dietary non-compliance, the new neurological signs, and any phenylalanine level obtained.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Managing the crisis at home or as a routine GP / behavioural issue
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Continued phenylalanine neurotoxicity progresses to seizures and irreversible brain injury

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Same-day GP without metabolic bloods or metabolic-team involvement — insufficient for a metabolic emergency with new neurological signs

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling 000 when urgent in-person metabolic/ED assessment would suffice — acceptable and safe given the risk, but an ambulance may not be needed without life-threat signs

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact phenylalanine level and the degree of neurotoxicity cannot be measured via telehealth
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as an evolving metabolic emergency, escalate for urgent in-person measurement and management, and safety-net conservatively
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassure and manage remotely, or attribute the presentation to behaviour, psychiatric illness or drugs
